title: Surveillance of shigellosis (Shigella) - Canada.ca
content:
## Surveillance of shigellosis ( Shigella )
Learn about shigellosis surveillance.
- Number of cases of shigellosis in Canada
- How Canada monitors shigellosis

## Number of cases of shigellosis in Canada
In recent years, approximately 880 cases of shigellosis have been reported annually in Canada.

## How Canada monitors shigellosis
Canada uses different surveillance systems to monitor cases of food-borne illness. These include:
- the National Enteric Surveillance Program (NESP)
- the Canadian Notifiable Disease Surveillance System
Shigellosis is a nationally notifiable disease in all provinces and territories. Cases are reported to:
- provincial or territorial departments of health
- the federal government, if they meet the national case definition

## For more information
- Public health notices
- Canadian Notifiable diseases online
- National Enteric Surveillance Program (NESP)
